specialty: Enterprise dealer (new/used auto retail); not specialized in AR or CR
services: Digital Advertising (Search, Display, Social, Video, OTT/CTV)
typical_monthly_usd: $3,000-$8,000/mo management fee per rooftop typical for franchise dealer; large dealer groups (Asbury-scale) negotiate enterprise SaaS contracts [uncertain]
setup_fee_usd: Not published; enterprise onboarding likely waived or bundled into annual contract [uncertain]
min_ad_spend_usd: No public minimum; OEM co-op programs (Subaru Certified Digital, etc.) set floor via tier-program rules. Typical dealer media spend $5,000-$50,000/mo [uncertain]
contract_length_months: 12+ typical for enterprise SaaS; multi-year for top-100 dealer groups [uncertain]
cancellation_policy: Not published; enterprise contracts typically require 30-90 day written notice and may have minimum-term commitments [uncertain]
asset_ownership: Dealer typically retains Google Ads / Meta accounts under OEM compliance rules; CDP data and PureCars proprietary dashboards/Value Reports remain PureCars IP [uncertain]
pricing_transparency: Quote-only; no pricing published on website or G2
known_pain_points: Glassdoor employee reviews cite 'rush to bring new products to market that are white-labelled or buggy' — possible product quality concerns for dealers
Disorganization and inter-department communication issues reported internally (Glassdoor) — may translate to account-management churn
No public BBB complaint resolution record; not BBB accredited
Pricing opacity — no published rate card forces enterprise sales cycle
Tooling (Value Reports, CDP) optimized for new/used vehicle retail funnel; little public evidence of collision-repair fit
notable_clients: Asbury Automotive Group (top-5 US dealer group)
65 of top 100 North American dealer groups (per company claim)
pros: Deep OEM co-op program integration (Subaru, others) — eligible for manufacturer reimbursement
Premium media inventory access (Amazon, Disney+, TikTok, OTT/CTV) most SMB agencies cannot offer
Mature CDP with VIN-level shopper intent data
Scale — proven at 65 of top-100 dealer groups
AI-driven inventory merchandising and dynamic creative
cons: Built for franchise new-car dealer economics, not single-location collision or independent AR
Enterprise pricing model (likely $3K-$15K/mo per rooftop) far exceeds Koffman's Hibu-tier budget
Quote-only pricing creates evaluation friction for small shops
No collision-repair-specific case studies or DRP-aware service offering
Glassdoor signals product-quality and account-management stability concerns
Asset/data lock-in (proprietary CDP, Value Reports) similar to Hibu trap if relationship ends
koffman_relevance: Wrong fit for Koffman. PureCars is enterprise dealer software priced for franchise new-car rooftops and top-100 dealer groups, not single-location WI collision shops. Would not replace Hibu or Turnkey — PureCars wouldn't quote or onboard a shop Koffman's size, and even if they did the monthly cost would exceed Koffman's entire current marketing spend by 3-5x. Only relevant as a benchmark for what enterprise auto marketing capabilities look like (CDP, OTT/CTV, premium media) when contrasting with the SMB collision vendor landscape.
